Subtopic 5.3 Digital Business
Digital Business and Transformation Subtopic 5.3

Canva and Objective Setting & Value Creation

A Case Study of SaaS Accessible Value Creation in Graphic Design Disruption

Objective setting is a fundamental aspect of digital business strategy that helps organizations define clear goals, measure performance, and achieve sustainable growth. Digital business objectives should align with an organization's overall strategy while creating value for customers, employees, and other stakeholders. Value creation in digital business involves using digital technologies to improve customer experiences, increase operational efficiency, foster innovation, and gain a competitive advantage. Organizations with well-defined objectives are better positioned to adapt to changing market conditions and deliver long-term business success.[1]

Introduction & Business Challenge #

Canva is one of the world's leading cloud-based graphic design platforms and an excellent example of how clear objective setting and continuous value creation can drive digital business transformation. By simplifying graphic design, introducing AI-powered creative tools, and promoting collaboration through an easy-to-use platform, Canva has transformed from a startup into a globally recognized digital business serving individuals, educational institutions, and enterprises.[2]

Figure 1.1: Canva's cloud-based design platform provides users with an intuitive interface.

Canva was founded in 2013 in Sydney, Australia, by Melanie Perkins, Cliff Obrecht, and Cameron Adams. It provides a cloud-based design platform that enables users to create presentations, social media posts, posters, resumes, videos, logos, and marketing materials through an intuitive drag-and-drop interface.[2]

Before Canva entered the market, professional graphic design software was often expensive, complex, and required specialized technical skills. Small businesses, students, educators, and individuals found it difficult to create professional-quality designs without hiring designers or learning complicated software. Canva recognized this challenge and developed a digital platform that made graphic design simple, affordable, and accessible to everyone.

Objective Setting #

Organizations establish digital business objectives to improve customer satisfaction, expand market reach, encourage innovation, increase operational efficiency, and achieve competitive advantage. Clearly defined objectives provide direction for digital transformation initiatives and enable organizations to measure business performance effectively.

Canva established several strategic objectives to support its digital business growth:

  • Simplify graphic design: Enable beginners of all skill levels to design.
  • Increase global adoption: Drive rapid sign-up growth in regional markets.
  • Promote collaboration: Create shared canvas boards for enterprise teams.
  • Continuous innovation: Implement automated features like background removals.
  • Expand premium SaaS tiers: Upsell Canva Pro and Canva for Teams subscriptions.
  • Education and Nonprofits: Provide free tool access to classrooms and charity networks.
Figure 2.1: Canva's drag-and-drop editor simplifies graphic design for all skill levels.

These objectives enabled Canva to focus on customer needs while continuously improving its digital platform.

Value Creation #

Value creation refers to delivering meaningful benefits to customers through innovative products, services, and digital experiences. In digital business, organizations create value by improving convenience, accessibility, collaboration, affordability, and innovation.

Canva creates value by offering a user-friendly drag-and-drop editor that allows users to design professional content without advanced technical knowledge. The platform provides thousands of ready-made templates, stock images, icons, fonts, and design elements that significantly reduce design time.

To further enhance customer value, Canva introduced Magic Studio, a suite of automated creative tools that includes Magic Design, Magic Write, Background Remover, and image editing features. These innovations improve productivity while simplifying the design process. Canva also supports cloud storage, real-time collaboration, mobile accessibility, and seamless sharing across multiple digital platforms. Its freemium pricing model enables users to access essential features at no cost while offering advanced capabilities through premium subscriptions.[3]

Figure 2.2: Canva's Magic Studio integrates automated design tools to enhance productivity.
Figure 2.3: Real-time collaboration allows teams to co-edit projects simultaneously.
Figure 2.4: Canva integrates design templates and cloud sharing into a single platform.

Business Impact of Objective Setting & Value Creation #

Canva's clear business objectives and customer-focused value creation strategy have contributed significantly to its rapid global growth.[2]

Business Objective Canva Implementation Business Impact
Accessibility Intuitive drag-and-drop web dashboard editor. High global user onboarding rates across skill brackets.
Innovation Magic Studio automatic design tool integrations. Increased user workflow speed and creative efficiency.
Collaboration Real-time multiplayer canvas co-editing capabilities. Widespread adoption inside corporate/enterprise design teams.
Customer Value Vast template directories, stock catalogs, and fonts. Higher user satisfaction ratings and social sharing.
Revenue Growth Freemium tiers converting to Pro and Teams models. Robust monthly recurring revenue (MRR) SaaS business growth.

Strategic Comparison Matrix #

Traditional Graphic Design Software Canva Digital Business Platform
Requires professional design skills & training Beginner-friendly intuitive drag-and-drop interfaces
High upfront software purchasing costs Freemium entry tiers with upsell subscription structures
Local desktop software installation required Fully cloud-based accessible anywhere in browser
Limited or asynchronous project collaboration Real-time multiplayer team co-designing canvas
Manual vector design creation tasks Automated template layout and asset assistance
Complex nested keyboard menus user interface Simple, search-focused, drag-and-drop workspace
Figure 3.1: Canva's freemium model provides basic services with tiered paid upgrades.

Conclusion #

Canva demonstrates how effective objective setting and value creation contribute to successful digital business transformation. Canva established clear strategic objectives focused on accessibility, innovation, collaboration, and customer satisfaction while continuously creating value through cloud-based technologies and Artificial Intelligence.[1]

Through its easy-to-use design platform, AI-powered Magic Studio, freemium business model, and collaborative features, Canva has transformed graphic design into an accessible digital service for millions of users worldwide. This case study highlights that organizations achieve sustainable digital business success when strategic objectives are closely aligned with customer value creation and continuous innovation.[2, 4]

References #
